RAG Chatbot Advanced Test Document
This document is designed to test advanced RAG capabilities including multi-page parsing,
structured tables, and FAQ retrieval.

Section 1: Overview of RAG
Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) combines document retrieval with natural language
generation to provide more accurate and context-aware responses.

Section 3: Use Cases
RAG can be used in customer support, internal knowledge bases, and military intelligence systems
where accurate retrieval is critical.

Section 4: Frequently Asked Questions
Q: What is RAG?
A: RAG is a technique that combines retrieval with generation.
Q: Why use RAG?
A: It improves accuracy by grounding responses in real d

retrieval with generation.
Q: Why use RAG?
A: It improves accuracy by grounding responses in real data.
Q: What data can be used?
A: PDFs, databases, and structured documents.
Q: Does RAG reduce hallucination?
A: Yes, by using retrieved evidence.

Section 5: Mixed Content
